TRANSCRIPT
MODULE Three – The Seed Launch - Part Two
Executive Summary:
• 	In this lesson, we talk about your Prelaunch Plan.
• 	The launch itself is very, very simple. You don’t much Prelaunch content. It will
generally lead to a larger launch.
• 	Since you’re launching generally to a very small list you can usually be very informal.
You’ll probably have a good relationship with the list you might have a personal
relationship with a lot of people on the list.
• 	When you deliver the course you want it to be meaty but the actual Prelaunch
content it can be more pitch than content.
• 	The pitch for a Seed Launch is often Reluctant Hero.
• 	The Prelaunch can often be done 100% via email.
• 	You can also use a blog if you want, a simple blog post or two giving solid information
about your topic.
• 	It can be a video - If you’re going to do a video it’s often very, very informal where
you’re just sort of giving your reluctant group a walking through what’s coming.
Reluctant Hero Swipe
• 	“I love this stuff, I’m an expert, it’s been fun sharing what I’ve shared with you so far,
but I never wanted to teach it, I never wanted to take it any further, I wanted to
protect my time, I had too much going on, I was too involved with my family, I didn’t
want to turn this into my profession once and for all.”
• 	“But lots and lots of people keep asking me please tell me how you do that, please
spill the beans.”
• 	“So I finally decided to do this because of ______”
• 	“So I finally decided to actually teach what I know and spill the beans.”
!
!
!

TRANSCRIPT
MODULE Three – The Seed Launch - Part Two
Social Proof
• 	Social proof is automatically implied by that part in the Reluctant Hero about “so
many people ask me to teach this.”
• 	If you could have people either write back to you via email or if they make comments
on your blog post and you can show those replies or show those comments, then
that’s stronger proof
Scarcity
• 	Scarcity works great with these Seed Launches.
• 	“…And fortunately since this is going to be an interactive class, I’m going to have to
limit the number of attendees. So if you’re interested, signup right away before it fills
up.”
• 	Or “Hey we’ve got 300 people reading this, I know a lot of you are really interested.
Unfortunately, since it’s going to be an interactive class we’ve only got 40 spots. So if
you’re interested in this you’d better jump quickly or I’m sure it’s going to fill up
very, very quickly so you want to jump on board right away.”

TRANSCRIPT
MODULE Three – The Seed Launch - Part Two
TRANSCRIPT:
Okay, now we’re going to talk about your Prelaunch plan. The
launch itself is very, very simple you don’t much Prelaunch content and it will
generally lead to a larger launch. So let me just give you the basics here.
So the Prelaunch doesn’t have to be super-duper extensive when you’re
talking about one of these Seed Launches in general. Since you’re launching generally
to a very small list you can usually be very informal. You’ll probably have a good
relationship with the list you might have a personal relationship with a lot of people
on the list. So a Seed Launch is not like your Prelaunch content - it doesn’t have to be
meaty. When you deliver the course you want it to be meaty but the actual Prelaunch
content it can be more pitch than content. You want to remember that Reluctant
Hero Prelaunch because the pitch for a Seed Launch is often Reluctant Hero and that
tends to be a fairly understated pitch it’s not like a used car salesman type of pitch.
And I’ll talk about that Reluctant Hero in just a second.
The Prelaunch can often be done 100% via email where you’re sending us
some cool emails where you’re giving them a little bit of content but you’re basically
building up into the pitch. You can also use a blog if you want, a simple blog post or
two giving solid information about your topic. It can be a video. If you’re going to do
a video it’s often very, very informal where you’re just sort of giving your reluctant
group a walking through what’s coming.
In terms of the Reluctant Hero it’s for this type of Seed Launch it’s really
pretty simple. You’re talking about how, whatever your topic is, there’s teaching
guitar or growing tomatoes. “I really love this stuff, I love sending you emails and
teaching you about this material, it’s really become a labor of love for me but I never
really wanted to run out a full out class. I never wanted the responsibility of putting
together a product or really teaching a class for this but lately so many people have
been asking me about this they really wanted to learn how to grow big tomatoes or
they just keep on asking me how I can play the ‘F’ chord so well or how I can create
such create videos or how I can put on makeup so well or whatever it is, lots and lots
of people have asked me to teach this.”
“And so now because of” - and usually there’s some reason, “it’s because I’ve
heard from so many people, it’s because I just had this conversation with someone
!
!
!

TRANSCRIPT
MODULE Three – The Seed Launch - Part Two
and realized I had so much to share” or there’s got to be some reason, then because of
whatever the reason is “I finally decided to spill the beans and so I’m actually going
to teach this class once and for all.”
So that’s your basic Reluctant Hero pitch - it’s that “I love this stuff, I’m an
expert, it’s been fun sharing what I’ve shared with you so far, but I never wanted to
teach it, I never wanted to take it any further, I wanted to protect my time, I had too
much going on, I was too involved with my family, I didn’t want to turn this into my
profession.” So that’s the second piece.
The third piece is, “but lots and lots of people keep asking me please tell me
how you do that, please spill the beans.”
And then the fourth piece is, “so I finally decided to do this because of blank.”
Blank doesn’t have to be some huge reason it’s a fairly easy thing. “Because so many
people are asking me about this” or because I talk to so and so and I realized if I
shared this with them it would be so life changing for them or it could make such an
impact or I decided it was time to really just step up and take a leadership role.
There’s a lot of different ways you can fill in that blank it’s not too hard to do that.
And then the final thing - “so I finally decided to actually teach what I know
and spill the beans.”
So next up is an important part of the Seed Launch, social proof. Social proof
is automatically implied by that part in the Reluctant Hero about so many people ask
me to teach this. And then down here it’s stronger if you can actually show people are
asking you that or show people are excited. So if your first piece of Prelaunch, which
is often just a blog post or an email talking about how much you like to teach this and
how you’re thinking about teaching a class. If you could have people either if they
write back to you via email or if they make comments on your blog post and you can
show those replies or show those comments then that’s stronger proof, otherwise
you’re just implying it, which is fine if that’s all you’ve got but you’re just implying
about so many people have asked you to teach it.
Scarcity works great with these Seed Launches. Basically the way the scarcity
works is it’s pretty simple… “and fortunately since this is going to be an interactive
class I’m going to have to limit the number of attendees. So if you’re interested
signup right away before it fills up.” So just the way these launches are structured,
like I said, you’re usually going to limit it to 40, 50, 60 people and if you can generate

a list of 200 or 300 people then you can say, “Hey we’ve got 300 people reading this,
I know a lot of you are really interested. Unfortunately, since it’s going to be an
interactive class we’ve only got 40 spots. So if you’re interested in this you’d better
jump quickly or I’m sure it’s going to fill up very, very quickly so you want to jump on
board right away.”
So that’s standard the 10,000-foot view of your Prelaunch plan for a Seed
Launch.
